lessonActivities.title: Wish – Expressing Present Regrets! 😔
lessonActivities.content:
Wish expresses regrets about things we cannot change in the present or annoyances we want to stop.

Structures:

  • States/situations: wish + past simple (especially 'were' for all subjects)
  • Annoying actions/behaviour: wish + would + infinitive

Key: Use 'were' (subjunctive) instead of 'was' — even for I/he/she/it! 💡
examples: I don't speak French. → I wish I spoke French. / I wish I were better at languages.
He smokes too much. → I wish he would quit smoking.
functions: Complaining politely about other people's behaviour
Expressing desires for change in the present
lessonActivities.tips: 💡 Present Wish Rule: wish + past simple (states) or wish + would (annoying/repeated actions). Never use 'will'!

lessonActivities.title: Wish for Past Regrets! ⏰
lessonActivities.content:
For things we regret in the past, use wish + past perfect (or could have for lost ability/opportunity).

Structure: wish + had + past participle

  • I didn't save money. → I wish I had saved more money.
  • She lost her phone. → She wishes she hadn't lost it.

Alternative: wish + could have + past participle (lost opportunity) 🔄
examples: I missed the concert. → I wish I had gone to the concert.
We arrived late. → I wish we hadn't been late.
lessonActivities.tips: 💡 Past Wish Rule: wish + past perfect. Use 'hadn't' for negative regrets. No change possible — pure regret!

lessonActivities.title: If Only – Stronger, More Emotional Wishes! 💪
lessonActivities.content:
If only = wish but with much stronger emotion, frustration, or longing. It often appears with exclamation marks!

Structure: Same grammar as wish

  • Present: If only + past simple / were / would
  • Past: If only + past perfect

Use when feelings are intense! 😢
examples: If only I had known about the party earlier!
functions: Expressing deep regrets or strong desires
lessonActivities.tips: 💡 If Only Rule: Same tenses as wish, but more dramatic/emotional. Frequently used with !

lessonActivities.title: Wish / If Only with Could & Would – Abilities & Change! 🔮
lessonActivities.content:
Special uses:

  • Wish / If only + could = regret about present/future ability
  • Wish / If only + would = wish for change in behaviour/situation (often annoying)

Could = ability/opportunity; Would = change we want in others or things ✨
examples: I can't swim. → I wish I could swim. / If only I could swim!
The bus is always late. → I wish it would come on time.
functions: Expressing regrets about lack of ability
Wishing for change in annoying situations
Combining with modal verbs for nuance
lessonActivities.tips: 💡 Could vs Would: Could = I want the ability. Would = I want someone/something to behave differently.

lessonActivities.title: Wish vs If Only vs Hope – Important Differences! ⚖️
lessonActivities.content:
Wish / If only = unreal / imaginary / regrets (subjunctive tenses)

Hope = real future possibilities (normal tenses)

  • I hope it doesn't rain tomorrow. (possible)
  • I wish it wouldn't rain tomorrow. (I don't like rain — complaining)

Never use wish/if only for real future! ❌
examples: Hope: I hope you feel better soon. (real possibility)
Wish: I wish you felt better. (you don't feel well now)
If only: If only this headache would go away!
lessonActivities.tips: 💡 Key Rule: Wish/If only → unreal (past tenses). Hope → real future (will / present).

writingActivities.question: Write 6 sentences expressing present regrets or annoyances using 'wish' (include at least 2 with 'would' and 2 with 'were').
writingActivities.hint: Example: I wish people would be more polite on public transport.

writingActivities.question: Write 6 sentences about things you regret doing or not doing in the past using 'wish + past perfect'.
writingActivities.hint: Example: I wish I had learned to play the guitar when I was younger.

writingActivities.question: Rewrite the 6 sentences from activity 1 using 'if only' to make them more emotional.
writingActivities.hint: Example: If only people would be more polite on public transport!

writingActivities.question: Write 5 sentences wishing for abilities or skills you don't have using 'wish / if only + could'.
writingActivities.hint: Example: If only I could speak five languages fluently!

writingActivities.question: Write 6 sentences complaining about annoying habits/situations using 'wish / if only + would'.
writingActivities.hint: Example: I wish my roommate would stop leaving dishes in the sink.

writingActivities.question: Write a short paragraph (7–9 sentences) about your biggest past regret using a mix of wish + past perfect, if only, and reflection.
writingActivities.hint: Use emotional tone with 'if only' at least twice.

writingActivities.question: Write 5 sentences comparing 'wish' (neutral) and 'if only' (strong) versions of the same regret.
writingActivities.hint: Example: I wish I had visited Japan. / If only I had visited Japan!

writingActivities.question: Write 6 sentences using 'hope' for real future hopes and 'wish' for complaints about the same topics.
writingActivities.hint: Example: I hope it doesn't rain tomorrow. / I wish it wouldn't rain every weekend.

writingActivities.question: Create a short dialogue (8–12 lines) between two friends expressing regrets and wishes using wish, if only, and hope.
writingActivities.hint: One friend regrets the past, the other hopes for the future.

writingActivities.question: Write a reflective paragraph (8–10 sentences) about your life using at least 4 different wish/if only structures + 1 hope sentence.
writingActivities.hint: Mix present regrets, past regrets, ability wishes, and future hopes.

gameActivities.question: Present state regret:
gameActivities.hint: Subjunctive 'were' for all subjects in wishes.

gameActivities.question: Annoying behaviour wish:
gameActivities.sentence: I wish she ____ talking so loudly.
gameActivities.hint: Wish + would for things we want to change in others.

gameActivities.question: Past regret:
gameActivities.sentence: I wish I ____ that stupid comment.

gameActivities.question: Ability regret:
gameActivities.sentence: If only I ____ speak Japanese fluently.
gameActivities.hint: Wish/If only + could = regret about present ability.

gameActivities.question: Common error – avoid 'will':
gameActivities.sentence: I wish it ____ stop raining tomorrow.
gameActivities.hint: Never use 'will' after wish — use 'would' for complaints.

gameActivities.question: Wish vs Hope:
gameActivities.sentence: I ____ the weather is nice tomorrow. (real hope)

gameActivities.question: Past opportunity lost:
gameActivities.sentence: I wish I ____ accepted that job offer.

gameActivities.question: Strong complaint about weather:
gameActivities.hint: 'If only + would' = very strong emotional wish for change.

quizActivities.question: Which is correct for present regret?
quizActivities.hint: Present regrets use past simple after wish.
quizActivities.explanation: Wish + past simple for present unreal situations/states.

quizActivities.question: Correct subjunctive form:
quizActivities.explanation: Wish + had + past participle for past regrets.

quizActivities.question: 'If only' is best used when:
quizActivities.sentence: You want to express very strong emotion.

quizActivities.question: Which shows annoyance with someone's behaviour?
quizActivities.sentence: I wish you ____ talking during the movie!
quizActivities.explanation: Wish + would + infinitive = wish for change in behaviour.

quizActivities.question: Correct form for ability regret:
quizActivities.sentence: I wish I ____ play the piano.
quizActivities.hint: Wish + could = regret about present ability.
quizActivities.explanation: Could expresses lost or missing ability in wishes.

quizActivities.question: Which is NOT possible with 'wish'?
quizActivities.hint: Don't use present tense for future with wish.
quizActivities.explanation: Wish does not express real future — use 'hope' instead.

quizActivities.question: Common error – avoid:
options: I wish it would stop raining.
quizActivities.explanation: Subjunctive mood requires 'were' for all subjects.

quizActivities.question: Wish vs Hope – which for real future?
quizActivities.sentence: I ____ you pass your exam tomorrow.
quizActivities.hint: Hope = real possibility; wish = unreal regret.
quizActivities.explanation: Use 'hope' + present/future for real future events.

quizActivities.question: Strongest emotional expression:
quizActivities.explanation: 'If only' is more intense and dramatic.

quizActivities.question: Correct past regret:
quizActivities.hint: Past perfect after wish for past regrets.
quizActivities.explanation: Wish + had + past participle = unreal past.

spellingActivities.sentence: Wish expresses ____ about the past or present.

spellingActivities.hint: Verb mood for wishes and hypotheticals
Feeling of annoyance when things go wrong
Skill or power to do something

spellingActivities.sentence: I wish I hadn't missed that ____.

badges.name: Wish Master
badges.condition: Complete all wish and if only lessons
